summary: First iteration testing of InviteCraft (event invitation creator). All 18 backend pytest tests passed including auth, invitations CRUD, cross-user isolation, public share link, AI text (Claude Sonnet 4.6) and AI image generation (Gemini Nano Banana). End-to-end Playwright frontend flow succeeded: register -> templates -> editor (live canvas update, auto-save, tab switching, share link copy) -> public view -> dashboard card.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 350-line file; acceptable for MVP scale but consider splitting routers (auth, invitations, ai) as feature grows.
AI text generation strips only surrounding quotes; if the LLM returns markdown (**bold** or backticks), formatting will leak into UI. Consider a small sanitizer.
Auto-save PATCH is triggered on every keystroke via debounced effect; on very slow networks this could stack. Not blocking, but consider cancelling in-flight request.

CORS is wide-open (*) with allow_credentials=True which is technically invalid per spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Not currently breaking since frontend does not send cookies (uses Bearer token).
JWT_SECRET has a default-looking value committed to .env; fine for preview but flag before production.
seed_data_creation: None. Tests create and clean up their own data (users + one invitation, invitation deleted at end).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, invitations CRUD, isolation, public link, AI text+image (real API hits, ~10s runtime total with xdist). Frontend flow validated end-to-end via Playwright including live canvas update (data-testid=invite-title reflects input-title in real time), auto-save chip, tab switching (tab-content/design/bg/ai), share link (clipboard writes /i/{share_id}) and PublicView rendering. Dashboard shows created invitation card. No open bugs.
